# Stock Price Forecasting with Machine Learning

This project investigates whether machine learning and deep learning models can improve short-term stock price predictions compared to traditional statistical models.

The study focuses on predicting the daily closing price and direction of Google (GOOG) stock using historical financial data.

Models implemented:
- Linear Regression
- ARIMA
- Random Forest
- LSTM Neural Network

The project includes feature engineering with technical indicators such as:
- Moving averages
- RSI
- Rolling volatility
- Lagged price features

Future development will include an interactive Streamlit application that allows users to input any stock ticker and generate forecasts.

Tools used:
Python, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-Learn, TensorFlow, yfinance, Streamlit
